description_long: This object consists of a set of five rectangular wooden storage boxes, each constructed from slats of wood with open tops and sides, allowing for ventilation and visibility of contents. The boxes are arranged in a row and display a range of wood tones, from dark brown to a lighter, more golden hue, suggesting either different wood types or varying degrees of weathering. Each box features a simple, utilitarian design with parallel horizontal slats forming the sides and base, and vertical corner supports for structural integrity. The interiors are empty and unobstructed, making them suitable for holding or transporting various items. The open construction and lack of lids or handles indicate easy access and stackability. These boxes are commonly found in urban environments such as markets, garages, or storage areas, where they serve as practical containers for goods or tools.
description: A set of five open wooden storage boxes with slatted sides and bases, displaying various shades of brown. The boxes are simple, sturdy, and designed for easy access and stackability.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open tops and slatted sides of all five boxes, highlighting their varying wood tones.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the narrow profile of the boxes, emphasizing the slatted construction and open ends.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the open tops and slatted sides, with subtle differences in wood grain and color.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and the alignment of the slats along the length of the boxes.
